source: NvChad - NvChad UI: NvimTree: how NvimTree opens files and its create/rename/delete/cut/copy keys

questions.q: With a file selected, what do Enter or o do?
options: Open it in a new buffer with the cursor on the first line
questions.why: Enter/o opens the file and places the cursor at line one.

questions.q: Which key opens a file in a new buffer but KEEPS the cursor in nvimtree?
questions.why: Tab is handy for opening several files at once.

questions.q: What do Ctrl+t and Ctrl+v do in NvimTree?
options: Ctrl+t opens in a new tab; Ctrl+v opens in a vertical split
both open in the same buffer
both open in a new tab
questions.why: Ctrl+t = new tab, Ctrl+v = vertical split, Ctrl+h = horizontal split.

questions.q: The a (add) key creates a FOLDER (not a file) when the name...
questions.why: A trailing / makes it a folder; otherwise a file is created.

questions.q: Which keys rename a selected file?
options: r (from the original name); Ctrl+r (regardless of the original name)
questions.why: r renames keeping the old name as a base; Ctrl+r renames from scratch.

questions.q: What does d do in NvimTree (with a y/n confirm)?
options: Deletes the selected file, or a folder with all its contents
questions.why: d deletes; all destructive actions confirm with y/n.

questions.q: Which trio cut, copy and paste in NvimTree?
questions.why: x cut, c copy, p paste; cut+paste moves files within the tree.

questions.q: What do y, Y and g+y copy to the clipboard?
options: y = file name, Y = relative path, g+y = absolute path
y = absolute path, Y = relative path, g+y = name
all three copy the file contents
y = name, Y = size, g+y = date
questions.why: y name, Y relative path, g+y absolute path.

options: disabled by default; enabled via override (see the Template Chadrc page)
questions.why: Enable git support by overriding M.nvimtree settings.
